title: Russia ceasefire violations — 2026-05-09
narrative: Trump announced a temporary three-day ceasefire (May 9–11) between Russia and Ukraine, brokered by the US, including a reciprocal prisoner exchange of 1,000 from each side. Public statements from the White House framed this as a diplomatic breakthrough and a sign of Russian good faith.
telemetry: • ISW (Institute for the Study of War) May 8 assessment: Russian forces continued offensive operations during the unilaterally-declared ceasefire window; Moscow accused Ukraine of violations while launching drones and missiles that killed more than 20 civilians on May 6.
• Zelensky directly stated Russia violated the ceasefire regime — strikes hit Kharkiv (2 injured, 7 homes damaged), Zaporizhzhia, Kherson.
• OSINTtechnical (X): "Russia has broken its Trump-brokered ceasefire agreement in Ukraine, carrying out major strikes tonight on multiple cities."
• Russia Review (May 1–8): Russia launched drones and missiles during Ukraine's unilateral "regime of silence" on May 6.
analysis:
Official sources say one thing. The evidence says another. The gap between the diplomatic announcement and the on-ground reality. What does satellite/thermal imagery show in the strike zones vs. what the White House described as agreement? Did the prisoner exchange actually occur, and what does AIS/fleet tracking show about Russian military positioning during the ceasefire window?

The pattern in the telemetry — 4 independent data points — points in a consistent direction that the official narrative does not acknowledge. When the official framing and the physical evidence are in contradiction, the evidence is the more reliable signal. The gap between what officials claim and what the data shows is the story.
